title: TC001-Navigate from Home to Portfolio via navigation links
description: Verifies that a visitor can move from the landing page to the portfolio page and see portfolio content render after a transition.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the navigation link for 'Website Showcase' (portfolio) to navigate to the portfolio page and then verify project content renders after the transition.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/nav/div/div/div[3]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-Primary content loads on each main route
description: Verifies that each key page route loads and renders its primary content area.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the About navigation link to load and verify the About page primary content.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/nav/div/div/div[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the Website Showcase (Portfolio) navigation link and verify the portfolio page primary content loads.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/nav/div/div/div[3]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'My Team' navigation link to load and verify the Services/My Team page primary content.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/nav/div/div/div[4]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'My Team' navigation link to load and verify the Services/My Team page primary content (use interactive element index 2097).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/nav/div/div/div[4]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Contact With Me' navigation link to load the Contact page and verify that the contact inquiry form is present.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/nav/div/div/div[5]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Contact With Me' navigation link (use the fresh interactive element at index 3339) to open the Contact page and verify the contact inquiry form is present.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/nav/div/div/div[5]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC005-Reduced motion: navigation still works and content renders
description: Verifies that with reduced motion enabled, the user can still navigate and content renders (without relying on heavy animations).
code:
import asyncio

async def run_test():

testError:
Reduced-motion preference could not be enabled because the site does not expose an on-page reduced-motion or accessibility toggle.

Observations:
- The site loaded successfully and is interactive: navigation links (Home, About, Website Showcase, My Team, Contact With Me) and other UI elements are present.
- A search for the text 'reduced motion' returned no results and no on-page accessibility toggle was found.
- The page was scrolled to the footer and no reduced-motion control or accessibility settings were visible.
- The site contains navigation to the portfolio ("Website Showcase") which is visible but the reduced-motion state could not be changed to verify behavior with animations disabled.
- Because no on-page control exists and browser-level preference could not be changed from this test environment, the reduced-motion verification could not be completed.

title: TC006-Sitemap route loads and supports navigation to pages
description: Verifies that the sitemap page loads and allows navigating to another page using sitemap links.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /sitemap (http://localhost:5173/sitemap).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/sitemap", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Home' link in the Main Pages section (element index 1466) to navigate to the Home page and then verify the primary content is displayed.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section/div/div/div/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Ensure the site navigates to the real Home page and verify its primary/hero content is displayed (click the main top navigation 'Home' and confirm the Home hero appears).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/nav/div/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /sitemap to retest clicking a sitemap link (Home) and verify the destination page's primary content appears.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/sitemap", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the sitemap 'Home' link on the /sitemap page to navigate to the Home page, then verify the Home primary/hero content is displayed.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section/div/div/div/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the sitemap 'Home' link (index 2909) on the /sitemap page to navigate to the Home page and then verify the Home hero/primary content appears.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div[2]/div/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /sitemap so the sitemap links can be clicked (next immediate action).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/sitemap", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the sitemap 'Home' link (index 5541) on the /sitemap page to navigate to the Home page and verify the Home hero/primary content appears.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section/div/div/div/div/div/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC007-Submit contact inquiry with all fields and completed reCAPTCHA
description: Verifies that a visitor can fill the inquiry form, complete reCAPTCHA, submit, and see an on-screen confirmation.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /contact (explicit path provided in test steps).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/contact", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill the contact form fields (name, email, WhatsApp, message), select a budget option, and submit the form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Demo Sender')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('demo.sender@example.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('+1 555 1234567')
        
        # -> Fill the message field with the automated message, select a budget option, then submit the form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/div[4]/textarea').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('This is an automated inquiry message for testing')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/div[5]/div/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the Send Message submit button (fresh index 2020) to submit the form, then check the page for a success confirmation message.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Select a project budget option, submit the form again, then check the page for a visible success confirmation message.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/div[5]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Your message has been sent').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
Submitting the contact form did not produce an on-screen success confirmation. The form was filled and submitted, but no visible confirmation message appeared on the page.

Observations:
- The contact form fields were filled: Name (Demo Sender), Email (demo.sender@example.com), WhatsApp (+1 555 1234567), and Message.
- A project budget option (100-500) was selected and the Send Message button was clicked.
- A dev-mode note '🛡️ Dev Mode: reCAPTCHA bypassed for local testing' is visible on the page.
- A search of visible page content did NOT find confirmation phrases such as 'thank', 'thank you', 'message sent', 'sent', 'success', or similar.
- The page continues to show the contact form and no on-screen success confirmation UI was observed after submission.

title: TC008-Contact form validation: missing email shows an error
description: Verifies that submitting the contact form without a required email shows a validation error.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Open the contact page by clicking the 'Contact With Me' navigation link (index 96).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/nav/div/div/div[5]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Fill the name and WhatsApp (again to ensure values), select a budget option, fill the message field leaving the email empty, submit the form, wait briefly, then extract visible page text to verify an email validation error is shown.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Demo Sender')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('+1 555 1234567')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Fill the message field (leaving email empty), submit the form, then extract visible page text to verify an email-required validation error is shown.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/div[4]/textarea').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('This message is submitted without an email')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the Send Message button to submit the form, wait for the response/UI update, then extract visible page text to verify an email-required validation error is shown.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC009-Contact form prevents submit when reCAPTCHA is not completed
description: Verifies that the form cannot be successfully submitted without completing reCAPTCHA and the user is prompted to complete it.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Contact With Me' navigation link to open the contact page and reveal the contact form (index 101).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/nav/div/div/div[5]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Please complete the reCAPTCHA').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC010-Contact form retains entered values after client-side validation error

code:

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the '100-500' budget option (index 2142) to select a budget before submitting the form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/main/div/section[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/div/form/div[5]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
